metadata.title: Censys Add Incident Comment
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered via HTTP request and is designed to be used as a sub-playbook by other Censys playbooks (CensysEnrichment, CensysEntityEnrichmentHost, CensysEntityEnrichmentCertificate, CensysEntityEnrichmentWebProperty, CensysAlertEnrichment). It receives enrichment data (host, web_property, certificate) along with the incident ARM ID and parent playbook name. The playbook processes each data type, extracts relevant fields (IP, autonomous system, WHOIS, location, DNS, services, threats, vulnerabilities, labels, software), formats them into HTML tables, and adds them as comments to the Microsoft Sentinel incident. It handles comment character limits (splitting into multiple comments if needed) and enforces a maximum of 100 comments per incident. The enrichment data is also ingested into Azure Log Analytics custom tables (Incident_Enrich_Host_Data_CL, Incident_Enrich_WebProperty_Data_CL, Incident_Enrich_Certificate_Data_CL) for historical analysis. The playbook includes comprehensive error handling and returns appropriate HTTP responses.
prerequisites: 1. This playbook is intended to be called as a sub-playbook by other Censys playbooks.
2. Ensure the parent playbook (CensysEnrichment, CensysEntityEnrichmentHost, etc.) is deployed and configured.
3. The Log Analytics workspace must be configured to receive custom logs.
postDeployment: 1. Authorize the Microsoft Sentinel and Log Analytics Data Collector API connections.
2. Configure the parent playbooks to call this sub-playbook using its HTTP trigger URL.
3. Ensure the playbook has appropriate permissions to add comments to incidents.

metadata.title: Censys Alert Enrichment
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered by a Microsoft Sentinel Alert. It extracts IP addresses, domains, and certificate file hashes (SHA256) from alert entities, then queries the Censys API to retrieve enrichment data for hosts, web properties, and certificates. The enriched data is ingested into Log Analytics Workspace custom tables (CensysHostAlert, CensysWebPropertyAlert, CensysCertificateAlert). If the alert is associated with an incident, the playbook invokes the CensysAddIncidentComment sub-playbook to add enrichment data as incident comments.
prerequisites: 1. Deploy the CensysAddIncidentComment playbook before deploying this playbook.
2. Obtain a Censys API token and store it in Azure Key Vault as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'.
3. Obtain the Censys Organization ID from your Censys platform account.
4. Create or identify an Azure Key Vault and note its name and Tenant ID.
5. Ensure you have a Log Analytics Workspace configured for Microsoft Sentinel.
postDeployment: **a. Authorize connections**
Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection.
1. Go to your logic app → API connections → Select keyvault connection resource
2. Go to General → edit API connection
3. Click Authorize
4. Sign in
5. Click Save
6. Repeat steps for Azure Sentinel and Log Analytics Data Collector connections
**b. Add Access policy in Keyvault**
Add access policy for the playbook's managed identity to read secrets from Key Vault.
1. Go to logic app → <your logic app> → identity → System assigned Managed identity and copy Object (principal) ID.
2. Go to keyvaults → <your keyvault> → Access policies → create.
3. Select Get and List permissions for Secrets. Click next.
4. In the principal section, search by copied object ID. Click next.
5. Click review + create.
**c. Assign Role for Microsoft Sentinel**
Assign Microsoft Sentinel Responder role to the playbook's managed identity.
1. Go to Log Analytics Workspace → <your workspace> → Access Control → Add
2. Add role assignment
3. Assignment type: Job function roles
4. Role: Microsoft Sentinel Responder
5. Members: select managed identity for assigned access to and add your logic app as member
6. Click on review + assign

metadata.title: Censys Alert Rescan
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered manually via HTTP request from a workbook or automation. It accepts input parameters including IOC Type (Host or Web Property), IP, Port, Protocol, Transport Protocol, Hostname, and Alert ID. The playbook initiates a rescan request to the Censys API, monitors scan status until completion, retrieves the updated asset data, and ingests the rescan results into Log Analytics. If the alert is associated with an incident, the playbook invokes the CensysIncidentEnrichment sub-playbook to add the rescan data as an incident comment.
prerequisites: 1. Deploy the CensysAddIncidentComment playbook before deploying this playbook.
2. Obtain a Censys API token and store it in Azure Key Vault as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'.
3. Obtain the Censys Organization ID from your Censys platform account.
4. Create or identify an Azure Key Vault and note its name and Tenant ID.
5. Ensure you have a Log Analytics Workspace configured for Microsoft Sentinel.
6. Configure the Censys workbook to trigger this playbook with required parameters.
postDeployment: **a. Authorize connections**
Once deployment is complete, authorize each connection.
1. Go to your logic app → API connections → Select keyvault connection resource
2. Go to General → edit API connection
3. Click Authorize
4. Sign in
5. Click Save
6. Repeat steps for Azure Monitor Logs and Log Analytics Data Collector connections
**b. Add Access policy in Keyvault**
Add access policy for the playbook's managed identity to read secrets from Key Vault.
1. Go to logic app → <your logic app> → identity → System assigned Managed identity and copy Object (principal) ID.
2. Go to keyvaults → <your keyvault> → Access policies → create.
3. Select Get and List permissions for Secrets. Click next.
4. In the principal section, search by copied object ID. Click next.
5. Click review + create.
**c. Configure Workbook Integration**
Configure the Censys workbook to call this playbook with the HTTP POST URL and required parameters.

metadata.title: Censys Entity Enrichment - Certificate
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered automatically when a FileHash entity is detected in a Microsoft Sentinel incident, based on an automation rule. Upon triggering, it extracts the FileHash (certificate fingerprint) from the entity and queries the Censys API (v3) to retrieve detailed certificate information including location, autonomous system, WHOIS data, and associated services. The playbook retrieves the Censys API token securely from Azure Key Vault, performs the API call with retry logic and comprehensive error handling for HTTP status codes (200, 401, 403, 404, 422). Upon successful data retrieval, the certificate data is ingested into Azure Log Analytics (CensysCert_CL table) for historical analysis and reporting. The playbook then retrieves the associated incident and invokes the CensysIncidentEnrichment sub-playbook to add the enrichment data as a comment to the incident. If no incident is associated, the playbook terminates successfully after data ingestion.
prerequisites: 1. Deploy the CensysIncidentEnrichment playbook before deploying this playbook.
2. Create an Azure Key Vault and store your Censys API token as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'.
3. Obtain your Censys Organization ID from the Censys platform account settings.
4. Configure an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook when FileHash entities are detected.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the playbook's managed identity 'Key Vault Secrets User' role on the Azure Key Vault.
2. Authorize the Microsoft Sentinel, Key Vault, and Log Analytics Data Collector API connections.
3. Create an automation rule in Microsoft Sentinel to trigger this playbook for FileHash entities.
4. Ensure the CensysIncidentEnrichment playbook is deployed and accessible.

metadata.title: Censys Host History
metadata.description: This playbook is triggered manually via HTTP request, typically invoked from a Microsoft Sentinel workbook. It retrieves historical timeline data for a specified host (IP address) from the Censys API (v3) within a given time range. The playbook accepts start_time, end_time, and host parameters in RFC3339 format. NOTE: Start time must be the timestamp closest to the current time (more recent) and End time must be the timestamp furthest from the current time (older). The playbook validates input parameters to ensure times are not in the future and start_time is not greater than end_time. It retrieves the Censys API token securely from Azure Key Vault, then queries the Censys host timeline endpoint with pagination support (up to 10 pages). The playbook processes various event types including service_scanned, endpoint_scanned, location_updated, route_updated, and whois_updated events. Each event is enriched with UI links to the Censys platform for detailed investigation. The collected history data is ingested into Azure Log Analytics (Censys_Host_History_Data table) for analysis and reporting. Comprehensive error handling is implemented for HTTP status codes (200, 401, 403, 404, 422).
prerequisites: 1. Create an Azure Key Vault and store your Censys API token as a secret named 'Censys-Access-Token'.
2. Obtain your Censys Organization ID from the Censys platform account settings.
3. Deploy the Censys Host History workbook to invoke this playbook.
postDeployment: 1. Grant the playbook's managed identity 'Key Vault Secrets User' role on the Azure Key Vault.
2. Authorize the Key Vault and Log Analytics Data Collector API connections.
3. Configure the Censys Host History workbook with this playbook's HTTP trigger URL.
4. Ensure the Log Analytics workspace is configured to receive custom logs.

Please configure this logic app first and keep it enabled in order to use this workbook.","style":"info"},"name":"text - 5"},{"type":1,"content":{"json":"## Censys Rescan Workbook

**Purpose**: Rescan indicators (Hosts or Web Properties) to get fresh threat intelligence from Censys.

### Setup (2 steps)
1. **Select Environment**: Choose your Subscription ID and Resource Group
2. **Choose Target Type**: Pick "Host" or "Web Property" from the filter

### Enter Target Details

**For Host Scans:**
- IP Address (e.g., 192.168.1.100)
- Port (e.g., 80)
- Protocol(e.g., HTTP)
- Transport Protocol (from dropdown)

**For Web Property Scans:**
- Domain (e.g., suspicious-site.com)
- Port (e.g., 443)

### Run Rescan
1. Click **"Run Playbook"** and select **Incident** or **Alert**
2. In the side panel, click **"Get Rescan Data"**
3. CensysRescan Logic App processes your request automatically
4. **Results Delivery**:
   - **Incident Rescan**: Comments added directly to your Incident
   - **Alert Rescan**: If alert has an associated Incident, comments added to that Incident
   - **All Results**: Data also ingested into your Log Analytics Workspace

### Important Notes
- **Comment Requirements**: No comments added if Host/Domain isn't found in the Incident
- **Progress Tracking**: Monitor Logic App status:
  - **Incident Rescan**: Check CensysRescan Logic App
  - **Alert Rescan**: Check CensysAlertRescan Logic App

Please configure this logic app first and keep it enabled in order to use this workbook.","style":"info"},"name":"text - 2"},{"type":1,"content":{"json":"## Ad-Hoc IOC Lookup Workbook

**Purpose**: Quickly investigate indicators (Host, Web Property, Certificate) without creating incidents or alerts.

### Quick Setup (3 steps)
1. **Select Environment**: Choose Subscription ID and Resource Group
2. **Choose Indicator Type**: Pick Host, Web Property, or Certificate
3. **Enter Indicator Value**: Input the IOC you want to investigate

**Indicator Format Examples:**
- **Host**: `192.168.1.100` or `10.0.0.1`
- **Web Property**: `1.1.1.1:443` or `abc.com:80` (host:port format)
- **Certificate**: Certificate fingerprint or SHA hash

### Run IOC Lookup
1. Click **"Submit"**
2. In side panel, click **"Get Ad-Hoc Lookup Data"**
3. Wait for **"Click on refresh icon to check data availability"** message
4. Click refresh icon until you see **"Click here to populate data"**
5. Click the message to view all indicator data panels


### Important Notes
- **New Environments**: First-time lookups take 10-20 minutes (Microsoft Sentinel delay)
- **Hard Refresh**: Required before each new IOC lookup for proper panel population
- **Data Range**: Results include last 1 hour only
- **Progress Tracking**: Monitor CensysIOCLookup Logic App run status for real-time progress updates

Please configure this logic app first and keep it enabled in order to use this workbook.","style":"info"},"name":"text - 1"},{"type":1,"content":{"json":"## Censys Host History Workbook

**Purpose**: Pull historical data for a specific host to investigate past activity and changes over time.

### Setup (2 steps)
1. **Select Environment**: Choose your Subscription ID and Resource Group
2. **Enter Time Range & Target**:
   - **Start Time**: When to begin data collection (e.g., 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z)
   - **End Time**: When to stop data collection (e.g., 2026-01-02T00:00:00Z)
   - **Host**: Target IP address

**Time Format Tips:**
- Use format: YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z
- Example: Jan 1, 2026 → 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z
- For full day data: Set end time to next day's start

### Run History Pull
1. Click **"Submit"** (activates when all fields are filled)
2. In side panel, click **"Get Host History Data"**
3. CensysHostHistory Logic App processes your request
4. Data appears in your Log Analytics Workspace

### What You Get
- Complete host activity history for your time range up to 1000 events.

### Important Notes
- **Processing Time**: Larger time ranges require more processing time
- **Data Destination**: Results are sent to Log Analytics Workspace
- **Progress Tracking**: Monitor CensysHostHistory Logic App run status for real-time progress updates
- **Record Limits**: If more than 1,000 records exist, only the first 1,000 are displayed in the workbook. Use Censys platform for complete dataset exploration
